nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated benthic species; inhabits coral and rocky reefs inshore, typically at depths of 10–20 m. Associated with caves, crevices, and sheltered overhangs.

diet.notes: Has been documented feeding on meaty foods such as krill, shrimp, and small fish. In captivity, generally takes frozen meaty preparations; live saltwater feeder shrimp or similar are sometimes used to entice newly introduced specimens. Will consume small fish or crustaceans that fit in its mouth.

goodWith: robust fish too large to be swallowed
avoidWith: small fish that fit in the mouth

summary: The Blue Tip Longfin Grouper (Paraplesiops poweri), also known as the Northern Blue Devil, is a rare Australian roundhead (Plesiopidae) noted for its elongated grayish body with strikingly blue-edged fins, generally advised for intermediate keepers with a cave-rich system.
careNotes:
Paraplesiops poweri is a member of the Plesiopidae (roundheads), a family of secretive, cave-associated marine fish endemic to the Indo-Pacific and Australian waters. It is closely related to the Western Blue Devil (P. meleagris) but is found in more northerly tropical and subtropical Australian waters. It is described as a rare find in the aquarium trade.

The species has an elongated, grayish body with large fins outlined in blue — distinctive colouration that gives rise to the trade names 'Blue Tip Longfin Grouper' and 'Northern Blue Devil'. Despite being marketed under the 'grouper' trade name, this species is a roundhead (Plesiopidae), not a true grouper (Serranidae).

Adult size is contested between sources: LiveAquaria cites a maximum of 7.5 inches (~19 cm total length), while FishBase records a maximum of 8.3 cm standard length (~10 cm total length). These figures differ by roughly 2x, which likely reflects either measurement type (SL vs TL), geographic population variation, or data provenance differences. The range of 10–19 cm is used here; this data point is flagged for review. The congener P. meleagris reaches approximately 33 cm, so 19 cm would be a plausible upper bound for this smaller tropical species.

Temperature range is based on averaging the LiveAquaria recommendation (65–75°F = 18.3–23.9°C) and the FishBase subtropical range (20–26°C), yielding approximately 19–25°C. Note that this species appears to prefer somewhat warmer water than the temperate P. meleagris, consistent with its more northerly distribution.

The species is described as cave-dwelling and benthic. It generally prefers to shelter among rockwork, crevices, and overhangs, and may be cryptic and less active during the day. Providing ample hiding spaces with live rock is generally advised.

Regarding temperament: LiveAquaria labels the species 'peaceful' but also notes in the product description that it is aggressive and will consume small fish or crustaceans that fit in its mouth whole. A 'semi-aggressive' classification has been used here to reflect this dual nature — essentially peaceful toward fish too large to eat, but opportunistically predatory toward small tankmates.

Reef safety is listed as 'with caution': it will not typically nip corals but will hunt mobile invertebrates and small fishes. FishBase notes paternal egg-guarding behaviour (males guard the eggs), suggesting captive breeding may be possible with appropriate setup.

Tank size: LiveAquaria recommends 70 US gallons (~265 L). Given the species' territorial nature, cave-dwelling behaviour, and need for structured rockwork, this has been rounded up to 300 L on the nice-values list as a cautious minimum.
breedingNotes: FishBase records that males exhibit paternal care, guarding eggs after spawning. Details of captive breeding have not been widely documented in the hobby literature. Breeding is generally considered difficult given the rarity of this species in the trade.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary care sheet. Recommends 70 gal minimum (~265 L), max size 7.5 inches (~19 cm), pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, temperature 65–75°F (18.3–23.9°C), care level moderate, reef compatibility 'with caution', diet carnivore on meaty foods. Species listed as rare in the trade.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked taxonomy (Plesiopidae, Ovalentaria), max length (8.3 cm SL male/unsexed — significantly smaller than LiveAquaria figure; flagged as needs_review), depth (usually 10–20 m), native distribution (SW Pacific: Australia), subtropical temperature range (20–26°C preferred 24.2–27.8°C), paternal egg-guarding reproduction, IUCN Least Concern (2022), trophic level 3.6 (carnivore). Reef2Reef secondary (403) and BlueZoo Aquatics (product not found) both failed; FishBase used as primary secondary.

fish.breedingNotes: Males guard eggs after spawning (paternal egg-guarding). Captive breeding rarely documented; considered difficult due to rarity in the trade.
